mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the industrial production index signal rising manufacturing utilization and operating leverage, which translate into higher near-term earnings and drive positive 20-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive following INDPRO month-over-month increases in more than 50% of rolling 36-month instances.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate at or below 0.10 percent anchors the front end of the yield curve at the effective lower bound, forcing investors out the risk curve and generating positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25 percent occurring alongside an unemployment rate above 5 percent indicates monetary policy is actively offsetting labor market slack; this combination supports corporate margins and drives positive 20-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns during months with FEDFUNDS below 0.25% and UNRATE above 5% are negative in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating consumer price inflation, month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL raise expected policy rates and equity risk premia, producing negative 20-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following CPIAUCSL acceleration triggers are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls signal expanding labor income and aggregate demand, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive 5-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not positive in more than 50% of rolling 24-month instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month retail sales growth exceeding 1.5 percent signals a consumer demand burst that outpaces current production, leading to positive earnings surprises and positive 20-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than 50% of months when RSAFS growth exceeds 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1,400 thousand units combined with a federal funds rate below 0.25 percent signal that accommodative monetary policy is transmitting into residential investment, driving positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns through construction and materials demand.
failure_condition: The joint condition of HOUST above 1,400 and FEDFUNDS below 0.25% fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of months, or the interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 6.0 percent signals labor market normalization that supports consumer spending confidence and reduces recession probability, generating positive 5-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when UNRATE is below 6.0%.
